body:
Title: CRA Form T691 2025 Alternative Minimum Tax.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/t691/t691-25e.pdf

CRA T691 2025 calculates federal alternative minimum tax. Normalized numeric values used by the encoded formulas include 0.30, 0.40, 0.50, 0.130435, 0.275362, 177882.00, 0.205, 0.80, 0.48, 0.666666, and 0.55. Part 1 calculates adjusted taxable income and the minimum amount. Line 4 subtracts film property line 3 from line 2 and floors at zero. Line 7 subtracts rental and leasing line 6 from line 5 and floors at zero. Line 11 adds tax shelter and limited-partnership lines 8 to 10. Line 12 adds lines 1, 4, 7, and 11. Line 14 uses resource property and flow-through shares from line 6 of Part 9. Line 23 uses line 22 if line 20 is positive, or the lesser of line 21 and line 22 if line 20 is negative. Line 26 multiplies line 24 minus line 25 by 30%, encoded as 0.30. Line 32 subtracts line 31 from line 30 and floors at zero; line 31 uses 40%, encoded as 0.40. Line 33 adds line 27 and line 32. Line 44 adds line 34, line 38, and line 43. Line 53 adds line 47, line 50, line 51, and line 52. Line 63 adds lines 54 to 62. Line 80 multiplies line 78 by 50%, encoded as 0.50. Line 82 adds line 80 and line 81. Line 84 uses 13.0435%, encoded as 0.130435. Line 85 uses 27.5362%, encoded as 0.275362. Line 87 uses 40%, encoded as 0.40. Line 93 subtracts line 92 from line 83 and floors at zero. Line 94 is the basic exemption, $177,882.00. Line 95 subtracts line 94 from line 93 and floors at zero. Line 97 multiplies line 95 by 20.5%, encoded as 0.205. Line 98 multiplies line 33800 by 50%, encoded as 0.50. Line 99 multiplies line 34900 by 80%, encoded as 0.80. Line 103 subtracts line 102 from line 97 and floors at zero.

Part 2 calculates basic federal tax. Line 5 subtracts line 4 from line 1, line 6 is minimum tax carryover applied from Part 8, and line 7 is basic federal tax. Part 3 calculates regular net federal tax payable. Line 4 uses federal surtax on business income earned outside Canada, including 48%, encoded as 0.48, and T2203 allocation or non-resident/deemed-resident rules. Line 15 calculates regular net federal tax payable after foreign, logging, political, investment, and labour-sponsored funds tax credits. Part 4 calculates special foreign tax credit. Line 6 multiplies total foreign income by 20.5%, encoded as 0.205. Line 7 multiplies non-business income tax paid to a foreign country by 66.6666%, encoded as 0.666666. Note 13 says Quebec residents multiply business income tax paid by 55%, encoded as 0.55. Line 11 is the greater of federal foreign tax credit from Part 3 line 7 and line 10.

Part 5 calculates the obligation to pay AMT. Line 3 subtracts special foreign tax credit from the minimum amount and floors at zero. Line 11 determines whether the taxpayer is subject to AMT. Part 6 calculates federal tax payable under AMT. Line 7 combines basic federal tax and the T1206 line 22 adjustment. Line 11 uses the 48% surtax and the same T2203/non-resident logic. Line 14 is entered on line 41700. Part 7 calculates additional taxes paid for minimum tax carryover. Part 8 applies a minimum tax carryover from previous years against 2025 tax payable; line 12 is entered on line 40427 and line 17 is total minimum tax carryover available for 2026. Part 9 line 6 subtracts resource income lines 2 to 4 from line 1 and floors at zero.
